dataset: EPCRA Tier II Chemical Inventory Data

epcra_tier2_data.xml: <casNumber>7782-50-5</casNumber>; also verified via web search (CDC NIOSH, ChemicalBook, NOAA CAMEO Chemicals all confirm 7782-50-5)
epcra_tier2_data.xml contains: facilityName → 'Facility Name', chemName → 'Chemical Name', maxAmount → 'Maximum Daily Amount'
epcra_tier2_data.xml contains only one facility: <facilityName>Green Valley Water Facility</facilityName>. The claimed positive matches do not exist in the seed data.

epcra_tier2_data.xml contains only Green Valley Water Facility. Neither 'Cold Storage Logistics' nor 'Industrial Solvents Inc' appear in the data, which is consistent with the claim that they should NOT be in the export.
Task setup script (setup_task.sh) and export script (export_result.sh) both reference this path. This is a Windows file system path in the CAMEO environment.

pi_items.source: README.md states 'Real data loaded (NOAA Tier II XML: Green Valley Water Facility, 2 chemicals, 4 contacts)' but uses fabricated facility/contact names. The XML format is based on NOAA standards but the actual facility data is fictional.
epcra_tier2_data.xml contains exactly one <facility> element with <facilityName>Green Valley Water Facility</facilityName>

pi_items.verified_value: 2 chemicals: Chlorine (CAS 7782-50-5, 20000 lbs max) and Fluorosilic Acid (CAS 16961-83-4, 45000 lbs max)
pi_items.source: epcra_tier2_data.xml contains 2 <chemical> elements in Green Valley Water Facility

privileged_info_summary: The task queries for Chlorine (CAS 7782-50-5) chemical inventory in CAMEO Data Manager using EPCRA Tier II synthetic data. The seed data contains one facility (Green Valley Water Facility) with Chlorine inventory at 20,000 lbs maximum amount. The required export columns are Facility Name, Chemical Name, and Maximum Daily Amount. CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: The metadata claims that three positive match facilities (Water Treatment Plant A, Community Pool, Agri-Chem Supply) should be found in the export, but these facilities do not exist in the seed data. The only Chlorine facility in the data is Green Valley Water Facility, not any of the claimed positive matches. This represents a fundamental metadata error where the verifier expectations do not align with the actual seed data.
